description: A PWM motor that can be controlled by a PWM signal

config.description: The pin number to control the motor
PWM frequency in Hz. Most H-bridge motor drivers want 15000-25000 (above audible, within driver spec). Default 20000.
Ramp time for speed changes
Maximum PWM duty (0-255) corresponding to SPEED=1.0
Minimum PWM duty (0-255) corresponding to SPEED=0.0
Invert pin polarity at the output. Off = motor at full when pin pulled high (active-high driver). On = motor at full when pin pulled low (active-low driver, e.g. high-side P-channel MOSFET).

description: A servo motor controlled by a PWM signal, interpreted as a position

config.description: The pin number to control the servo
Servo angle (degrees) corresponding to POSITION=0.0
Servo angle (degrees) corresponding to POSITION=1.0

description: Two-servo arm aiming a laser/LED dot at a wall. ANGLE (p1) controls the dot's position on the wall as an angle from straight-out from the base. OFFSET (p2) controls the base servo angle — i.e. where the arm is pointing. At OFFSET=middle the arm points directly away from the wall; the upper servo is computed to keep the dot on target as OFFSET sweeps, so the beam takes different paths through space but the dot stays put. See gui/servo_arm_sim.html for the kinematics.

config.description: GPIO for the base servo
GPIO for the upper servo (mounted on the arm tip)
Base servo angle (°) at OFFSET=0.0 — one arm-swing extreme
Base servo angle (°) at OFFSET=1.0 — other arm-swing extreme
Lowest safe mechanical angle (°) for the upper servo — the firmware clamps here if the computed angle goes below
Highest safe mechanical angle (°) for the upper servo — the firmware clamps here if the computed angle goes above
Distance from the base pivot to the wall (mm)
Arm length from base pivot to upper servo (mm)
Beam angle (°) at ANGLE=0.0 — 0 is straight out from base, negative sweeps to one side of the wall
Beam angle (°) at ANGLE=1.0 — 0 is straight out from base, positive sweeps to the other side

description: A brushless motor driven through a BLHeli (or other PWM/PPM-compatible) ESC. The ESC sees standard hobby-servo style pulses, 1000 µs = zero throttle, 2000 µs = full. At init the ESC sees a sustained MIN_PULSE_US for ARM_DURATION_MS before any throttle is accepted. SAFETY: spinning brushless motors with no propeller/wheel attached can hit dangerous speeds at modest throttle — set MAX_PULSE_US to 1100-1300 µs while bench testing.

config.description: The GPIO driving the ESC signal wire
Pulse width (µs) corresponding to THROTTLE=0.0 (zero throttle / arm position). Standard ESCs: 1000 µs.
Pulse width (µs) corresponding to THROTTLE=1.0 (full throttle). Standard ESCs: 2000 µs. Lower this (e.g. to 1200) as a safety cap while testing.
PWM update frequency in Hz. 50 is the universal default; BLHeli_S typically accepts up to ~500.
How long the ESC must see MIN_PULSE_US at startup before it accepts throttle. 2 s is conservative; some ESCs arm in well under 1 s.
Software ramp time for throttle changes (0..1 in this many ms). Independent of the ESC's own ramping.

description: A continuous-rotation servo. SPEED 0.0 = full reverse (MIN_SPEED), SPEED 0.5 = rest (REST_SPEED), SPEED 1.0 = full forward (MAX_SPEED).

config.description: The pin number to control the servo
Servo value for full reverse, SPEED=0.0
Servo value for full forward, SPEED=1.0
Servo value at rest, SPEED=0.5
